content: Do we have any method for doing unaligned 32-bit reads, either via the kernel get_unaligned_le32 or something more rust?
oh I see asahi has a struct that might be useful
okay hacked something up with the asahi U32 type in an unsafe seems to work for now
I wasn't sure what from std we could use, if we can use std::ptr I should probably try that then
thanks that seems to have worked great!

std is the most common search engine link for most docs, but more or less everything in std that doesn't involve allocation or OS interfacing is available in core https://doc.rust-lang.org/core/index.html (I usually just replace std with corein the URL to see if something exists)
